data.title: Red Robin Gourmet Burgers perks up with activist investors on the prowl
data.description:
JCP Investment Partnership increased its active stake in Red Robin Gourmet Burgers (NASDAQ:RRGB) to 11.3% from 8.7% through a series of transactions across several related buying parties. The firm sees the restaurant stock as undervalued and representing an attractive investment opportunity.

JCP Investment Partnership is a private investment firm managed by JCP Investment Management, LLC, based in Houston, Texas. The partnership focuses on value-oriented investments and activist strategies, primarily targeting small to mid-cap public companies1. Led by James Pappas, JCP Investment Partnership has a track record of engaging with company management to drive shareholder value. JCP Investment has taken an activist investor role in Jamba, Potbelly (PBPB), and Fiesta Restaurant Group in the past.

The restaurant industry has seen a wave of activist actions, including at larger chains such as Starbucks (SBUX), Bloomin’ Brands (BLMN), and BJ's Restaurants (BJRI).

Shares of Red Robin Gourmet Burger (RRGB) have perked up over the last week, gaining more than 13%. Short interest on RRGB stands at 14.2% of the total float, which could add some volatility to the mix.

data.title: Truist Financial Q3 earnings beat as investment banking, trading income climb
data.description:
Truist Financial (NYSE:TFC) Q3 earnings exceeded the average analyst estimate as noninterest income climbed, on higher investment banking and trading income. Net interest income also rose, but failed to meet the Wall Street consensus.

For the last quarter of the year, the company expects adjusted revenue, on taxable equivalent basis, to decrease about 1.5% from Q3's $5.14B, implying adjusted revenue of ~$5.06B. That compares with the average analyst estimate of $5.10B. Adjusted expenses are expected to increase ~4% from Q3's $2.83B, implying ~$2.95B for Q4 2024 vs. the Visible Alpha estimate of $2.99B.

Q3 adjusted EPS of $0.97, beating the average analyst estimate of $0.91, rose from $0.91 in Q2 and $0.80 in last year's Q3.

"In the third quarter, we made considerable progress on driving revenue growth through our core banking business by adding new clients, deepening relationships, hiring and developing talented teammates, and investing in technology and infrastructure while maintaining strong expense discipline," said Chairman and CEO Bill Rogers.

Net interest income ("TE") of $3.66B, missing the Visible Alpha consensus of $3.67B, climbed from $3.58B in the prior quarter and $3.59B a year ago. Net interest margin ("TE") of 3.12% rose from 3.02% in the previous quarter and 2.92% a year ago.

Noninterest income of $1.48B vs. -$5.21B in Q2 and $1.33B in Q3 2023. The 3.1% Y/Y increase was driven by higher investment banking and trading income and other income.

Adjusted noninterest expense of $2.83B increased 0.9% Q/Q and dropped 2.3% Y/Y.

Adjusted preprovision net revenue for the quarter was $2.31B vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $2.14B, rising 4.4% Q/Q and 13.9% Y/Y.

Q3 provision for credit losses decreased to $448M from $451M in the prior quarter and $497M in the year-ago period. Net charge-off rate was 0.55% vs. 0.58% in Q2 and 0.51% in Q3 2023.

Average total deposits dipped to $384B from $388B in Q2.

Average total loans and leases held for investment stood at $303B in Q3 vs. $306B in Q2.

Truist (TFC) stock rose 0.8% in Thursday premarket trading.

Conference call at 8:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Truist Financial beats Q3 top and bottom line estimates

data.title: Taiwan Semi soars after AI demand boosts outlook; will not buy Intel foundry (update)
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor's (NYSE:TSM) stock jumped 8% in premarket trading on Thursday after the global foundry reported third-quarter results and guidance that topped estimates, amid surging demand for artificial intelligence chips.

TSM's results also assuaged sentiments on AI demand, after ASML's (ASML) results earlier this week sent shockwaves through the market. Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD) and Broadcom (AVGO) were just some of the chipmakers seeing premarket gains.

Third quarter net revenue grew 36% year-over-year to $23.50B (NT$759.69B, +39% Y/Y). Earnings per American Depositary Receipt was $1.94, compared to $1.29 in the third quarter of 2023.

High Performance Computing represented 51% of net revenue, up from 42% in the year-ago period. Its smartphone segment represented 34% of net revenue, versus 39% in the year ago period, and 33% compared to the second quarter of 2024.

Both top and bottom line numbers surpassed analysts' expectations.

"Our business in the third quarter was supported by strong smartphone and AI-related demand for our industry-leading 3nm and 5nm technologies," said TSM's Senior VP and CFO Wendell Huang.

Earnings call

TSM — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies, including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia and AMD — has seen a rise in demand for semiconductors used in AI applications.

"The demand is real, and I believe it's just the beginning" Chairman and CEO C.C. Wei said on the company's earnings call, referring to AI demand.

Other than AI, overall semiconductor demand has stabilized and has started to improve, Wei added.

Wei also said Taiwan Semiconductor has no interest in buying Intel's (INTC) foundry business.

Capex spending

Huang added that Taiwan Semi now expects 2024 capex to be slightly higher than $30B. Of that, between 70% and 80% will be for advanced process technologies; between 10% and 20% will be for specialty technologies; and about 10% will be spent for advanced packaging, testing, mask making and others.

Capital expenditures should be higher next year, Huang said.

Outlook

Revenue contribution from server AI processors will more than triple this year and account for a mid-teens percentage of total revenue in 2024, CEO Wei said. Full-year revenue should rise 30% year-over-year, Wei added.

For the fourth-quarter, TSM expects revenue to be between $26.1B and $26.9B (midpoint of $26.5B), versus the estimate of $25.02B.

Taiwan Semiconductor added it expects the first fab in Arizona to see volume production in 2025, while its second fab is expected to begin volume production in 2028. The third Arizona fab should start volume production by the end of the decade.

Analyst comments

"TSMC's earnings have likely calmed investor fears as they navigate the recent ASML earnings fallout," said JR Research, Investing Group Leader for Ultimate Growth Investing. "AI revenue is expected to remain robust, as TSMC raised its 2024 revenue growth outlook to 30% from mid-20% previously."

Earlier this week, ASML's third quarter bookings missed analysts' expectations, and the company's guidance sent shockwaves into the chip sector.

"TSMC's earnings beat was substantial. Despite the increasing mix shift towards the less profitable 3nm process node and rising electricity costs, TSMC outperformed the higher end of its 3Q gross margin guidance by more than two percentage points at 57.8%," said Seeking Alpha Analyst Livy Investment Research.

Livy noted this sets a strong foundation for TSM in maintaining its long-term gross margin target of 53% or more, despite emerging cost challenges, which include continued N5 to N3 tooling conversion costs, and overseas fab ramp up costs.

"Most importantly, we believe TSMC's consistent streak of margin improvements also complement its upcoming introduction of 2nm, which starts volume production next year," Livy added.

Q3 Revenue by Technology

TSM said 3nm process technology contributed 20% of total wafer revenue in the third-quarter, versus 6% in the year ago period, and 15% in the second quarter of 2024.

The 5nm process technology accounted for 32% of total wafer revenue, compared to 37% in the same period a year ago, and 35% in the second quarter of 2024. Meanwhile, 7nm accounted for 17% of total wafer revenue in the third quarter versus 16% a year earlier, and 17% in the second quarter of '24.

Advanced technologies (7nm and below) accounted for 69% of total wafer revenue.

Q3 Revenue by Geography

Revenue from China — Taiwan Semi's second-biggest market by revenue — accounted for 11% of the total net revenue in the period, down from 12% in the year-ago period and 16% in the second quarter of 2024.

North America accounted for 71% of total net revenue coming from it, compared to 69% a year earlier, and 65% in the second-quarter of 2024.

(This story has been updated to include additional information.)

data.title: GXO Logistics expands partnership with Henkel to include the beauty segment
data.description:
GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) announced on Thursday that it has extended and expanded its partnership in France with Henkel (OTCPK:HELKF) (OTCPK:HENKY).

The global contract logistics company manages storage, distribution, seasonal returns and value added-services for an array of Henkel brands across its laundry and home care segments and will now expand its services to include Henkel’s iconic beauty brands.

GXO (GXO) manages logistics for Henkel at its 60,000-square-meter site in the Orléans region of central France. With the addition of the beauty segment, GXO (GXO) will continue to add cutting-edge technology that delivers continued competitive efficiency on site, such as a pick to light solution and an inventory viewer, to support the transformation of picking processes and boost efficiency. GXO (GXO) also provides value-added services with co-packing activities and a transport tower control to serve Henkel’s customers in France as well as in the French overseas. Henkel brands served at the site include Super Croix, Xtra, Le Chat, Persil, Mir, Maison Verte, Decolor Stop, K2r, Catch, Fa, Taft, Palette and Schwarzkopf.

"Our unrivaled logistics experience and expertise will continue to optimize Henkel’s operations and provide enhanced service and quality to its customers. We look forward to deploying additional technologies to further boost efficiency and enhance safety," stated GXO executive Vincent Ricci.

Shares of GXO Logistics (GXO) were up 1% in premarket trading on Thursday to trade at a new 52-week high.

data.title: Blackstone Q3 earnings top consensus as investment activity picks up
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) turned in better-than-expected Q3 earnings on Thursday as investment activity picked up, and fund appreciation was its strongest in three years. Among its strategies, corporate private equity and infrastructure realized the best performances during the quarter.

Total assets under management of $1.11T at Sept. 30, 2024, topping the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.09T, increased from $1.08T at June 30. Fee-earning AUM rose to $820.5B from $808.7B at the end of Q2.

Net inflows of $40.5B increased from $39.4B in Q2.

Q3 distributable EPS of $1.01, beating the $0.93 consensus, rose from $0.96 in Q2 and $0.94 in last year's Q3.

Blackstone (BX) stock rose 1.5% in Thursday premarket trading.

"We invested or committed $54B in the quarter — the highest in over two years — and deployed $123B in the last twelve months since the cost of capital peaked," Chairman and CEO Stephen A. Schwarzman said.

Q3 fee-related earnings of $1.18B, or $0.96 per share, increased from $1.11B, or $0.91 per share, in the prior quarter and $1.12B, or $0.92 per share, in the year-ago period.

Net management and advisory fees were $1.79B, up from $1.78B in Q2 and $1.65B in last year's Q3.

At the end of the quarter, Blackstone (BX) had undrawn capital for investment, or dry powder, of $171.6B vs. $181.0B at the end of Q2. During the quarter, the company deployed $34.0B of capital and recorded realizations of $22.7B.

Total expenses of $1.90B rose from $1.63B in the previous quarter and $1.12B a year ago. Compensation and benefit expenses climbed to $1.44B from $1.21B in Q2 and $946.2M in Q3 2023.

Investment performance by segment (appreciation/gross returns):

  • Real Estate — Opportunistic: +1.1%; Core+: -0.5%;
  • Private Equity — Corporate Private Equity: +6.2%; Tactical Opportunities: +4.3%; Secondaries: +0.7%; Infrastructure: +5.5%;
  • Credit & Insurance — Private Credit: +3.6%; Liquid Credit: +2.4%.
  • Multi-Asset Investing — Absolute Return Composite: +2.2%.

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Blackstone beats Q3 estimates
